page: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 307 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark data positions the E8ds_v4 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU score is 1411.59, and the multi-core score is 7443.73. Memory bandwidth tests show 415.23 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 49.8 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database and web serving tests, the instance achieves 7.09 million operations per second on Redis SET operations and 7.32 GB/sec static web serving throughput. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak at 28.2 GB/sec, and medium-sized 7B LLM inference is poor, processing prompts at 14.31 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8ds_v4 offers a high density of memory relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial RAM without the cost of additional compute cores. The inclusion of 307 GB of local storage provides a resource-dense profile for data-heavy applications. This server is suitable for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier for most operations, with a Redis SET throughput of 7.09 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 7.32 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium-scale LLM inference. The high memory density and local storage capacity make this instance cost-efficient for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage, delivering average performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v4 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized VM with 64 GB RAM and 307 GB local storage, running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for database and caching workloads.

Average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving
